obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins and debug DMI signals, plus jtag_hash/jtag_unlock and debug outputs. Verify visible RTL actually gates debug operations with authorization rather than merely declaring unlock signals.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DTM or simulation debug interface
obligations.operation: issue debug/DMI requests, enter debug, reset, read/write debug state
obligations.object: debug capability, debug module registers, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization such as jtag_unlock or equivalent debug enable/lifecycle condition must mediate JTAG/DMI operations before they can request hart debug, reset, or access debug-module state
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs -> dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/SimDTM -> DMI/debug request signals -> debug module slave/master/debug_req outputs
state_conditions_to_check: reset value/initial state of jtag_unlock or debug authorization
whether SimDTM/SimJTAG/test paths differ in authorization
whether debug_mode_i bypasses or implies authorization
required_evidence_slots: subject JTAG/DTM source signals and instances
guard signal and its use site
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, observed path
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer lists signals/instances but not all instance port connections or internal debug module logic.

obligations.reason: The top module contains acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl, privilege input, many NoC peripheral ports, and at least one visible gate rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]. Check that all security-sensitive peripheral request paths receive equivalent mediation and no decode path bypasses acc_ctrl.
obligations.subject: software-visible NoC/AXI requester at riscv_peripherals
obligations.operation: read/write/configure/trigger via NoC or AXI-visible peripheral interfaces
obligations.object: boot ROM, fuse registers/data, reset controls, crypto peripheral interfaces, reglk/acct control registers, DMA wrapper interface
obligations.expected_guard: peripheral access should be gated by acc_ctrl indexed by priv_lvl_i and by any applicable lock bits for protected write/configuration paths
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals NoC/AXI request decode -> peripheral request enables -> rom_req/fuse_req/dma/crypto/reset/register-lock/accounting paths
state_conditions_to_check: updates to acc_ctrl and whether protected by privilege/lock
special aliasing in acc_ctrl_c assignment for j==5
read data mux behavior when access denied
required_evidence_slots: actual gating expression at use site
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer is truncated for later assignments and decodes, so exact protected paths require source inspection.

obligations.reason: reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl appear to control peripheral access and locks, and per-peripheral resets are exposed. Verify lock bits cannot be cleared or bypassed after being set, and that reset/control updates do not open unauthorized configuration windows.
obligations.subject: software-visible register interface and possible debug/test requester
obligations.operation: write, clear, update, reset, and use lock/access-control state
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl, peripheral reset bits rst_1..rst_13, DMA configuration registers
obligations.expected_guard: lock/control registers should be writable only by authorized privilege/access-control subjects, should be sticky or non-clearable when intended, and should protect all downstream writes they are meant to cover
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals register-lock/control writes -> reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl/rst_* -> peripheral wrapper lock inputs and reset outputs
state_conditions_to_check: after lock set then attempted clear
testmode/debug paths that may write locked objects
obligations.uncertainty: Need visible procedural blocks to distinguish intended sticky locks from normal writable controls.

obligations.reason: DMA has configurable source/dest/length/start registers and PMP-related allow/check registers. Check whether transfer validity is conditioned on PMP decisions for all accessed addresses, not only one endpoint or stale cached allow state.
obligations.subject: AXI software requester configuring DMA
obligations.operation: configure and trigger DMA read/write transfer
obligations.object: memory regions addressed by DMA source_addr_* and dest_addr_*, DMA start/valid controls
obligations.expected_guard: DMA start/valid generation must be gated by PMP allow/range checks for source and destination and by access-control/lock authorization for configuration writes
obligations.candidate_path: AXI-lite interface -> dma_wrapper registers -> dma core start/source/destination/length -> PMP check signals -> valid_o/transfer trigger
required_evidence_slots: PMP addr/access type supplied to pmp
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows PMP signals but not full FSM/condition expressions.

obligations.reason: dma_wrapper has reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, core_lock_reg, we_flag, and AXI write signals. Verify every writable DMA configuration register uses the same intended authorization/lock guard and that we_flag does not create an alternate write path.
obligations.subject: AXI software requester or external we_flag/test/control input
obligations.object: DMA control register, start_reg, length_reg, source_addr_lsb/msb_reg, dest_addr_lsb/msb_reg, core_lock_reg
obligations.expected_guard: writes to DMA control/address registers should be blocked when lock bits or accounting/access-control disable the subject; external we_flag/test inputs must not bypass the lock
obligations.candidate_path: dma_wrapper AXI register writes -> core_lock_reg/reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i/we_flag -> start/length/source/destination/done/end registers
state_conditions_to_check: when we_flag is asserted independent of bus write
obligations.uncertainty: Port named logic/we_flag in facts may be parser artifacts; source inspection needed.

obligations.reason: axi_lite_interface translates AXI requests into simple en/we/address/data signals without visible privilege ports. Check the top-level CLINT request path applies acc_ctrl/privilege guards before or after this interface and that denied accesses cannot update interrupt-control state.
obligations.subject: AXI requester accessing CLINT MMIO
obligations.object: CLINT timer/mtimecmp/software interrupt control registers and outputs timer_irq_o/ipi_o
obligations.expected_guard: CLINT register accesses should preserve requester/privilege mediation from the surrounding peripheral access-control layer; no AXI-lite handshake should generate en_o/we_o for unauthorized subjects or out-of-range addresses
obligations.candidate_path: CLINT axi_lite_interface ariane_axi -> address_o/en_o/we_o/data_o -> CLINT/timer/software interrupt registers
state_conditions_to_check: reset behavior of pending transaction state
obligations.uncertainty: CLINT register implementation is outside listed modules; obligation focuses on visible interface and top-level mediation.

obligations.reason: The top-level contains explicit fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata signals. Determine whether any bus, debug, or NoC requester can read fuse data and whether the response path is gated by appropriate authorization.
obligations.subject: software-visible bus/NoC requester or debug/test requester
obligations.object: fuse_rdata and fuse-addressed protected configuration/secret values
obligations.expected_guard: fuse reads should be restricted by privilege/access-control/lifecycle/debug authorization and should not be exposed on read muxes when access is denied
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata -> read-data mux/NoC response or peripheral output
state_conditions_to_check: stale fuse_rdata retained after denied request
required_evidence_slots: requester and address decode producing fuse_req
obligations.uncertainty: Only signal declarations are visible in facts; read mux details require source inspection.

obligations.reason: PMP logic is central to DMA/memory permission enforcement. Check range match calculations, entry priority, access type, and default allow/deny behavior under all configurations used in this scope.
obligations.subject: DMA or core memory access checker using PMP
obligations.object: PMP-protected memory regions checked by addr_i and allow_o
obligations.expected_guard: PMP allow decision must correctly enforce configured permissions for matching address ranges, including no-match/default-deny where required and privilege/access-type distinctions
obligations.candidate_path: pmp module entries -> pmp_entry match_o -> priority/match selection -> allow_o
state_conditions_to_check: access type read vs write vs execute
required_evidence_slots: use site in DMA or requester
obligations.uncertainty: Port facts for pmp include parser artifacts; exact config/access-type fields need source review.

obligations.reason: The top-level declares dm_master and dm_slave request/address/data signals and has tie-off assignments for some dm_master response signals. Verify whether debug can act as a bus master/slave and whether that path is either fully disabled or properly mediated.
obligations.subject: debug module/JTAG DMI requester
obligations.operation: read/write memory or peripheral space via debug bus path
obligations.object: system memory/peripherals reachable through debug master or debug slave AXI paths
obligations.expected_guard: any enabled debug bus-master path must be authorized and range-limited; if intentionally disabled, grants/valid/read-data tie-offs must make the path unreachable
obligations.candidate_path: debug module master/slave AXI signals -> dm_master/dm_slave assignments and NoC/fabric tie-offs -> protected memory/peripheral access
obligations.uncertainty: Some master response signals are tied off in facts, but request channel connectivity and slave path require inspection.
